project.name: opensre
project.description: Open-source AI SRE agent that automatically investigates production alerts and produces evidence-backed root-cause analyses by orchestrating a LangGraph pipeline over observability and infrastructure tooling (Datadog, Grafana, Coralogix, AWS/EKS, Kubernetes, Vercel, GitHub, etc.).

nodes.name: Alert Ingestion & Triage
nodes.summary: Captures raw alert payloads from Slack, Discord, Vercel pollers, CLI files, and remote HTTP triggers, normalizes them into a canonical schema, and uses an LLM extractor (with deterministic fallbacks) to turn unstructured signals into structured AlertDetails consumed by the investigator.

businessRules: Every investigation begins from a normalized alert; raw payloads are enriched with extracted name/namespace/severity before downstream nodes run
When the LLM extractor fails or returns an unusable payload, a deterministic fallback constructs AlertDetails directly from raw fields
Vercel and runtime self-investigation alerts are deduplicated by signature so the same failure does not trigger repeat investigations
crossDomainInteractions: Hands the structured alert to Incident Investigation for planning and execution
Receives configured ingestion sources (Slack, Discord, Vercel) from Onboarding & Configuration
Posts back to Findings Publishing for write-back into the originating GitLab MR / Slack thread

nodes.name: Incident Investigation
nodes.summary: The core LangGraph agent loop: a planner LLM picks investigation actions from the available tool surface, an executor fans out one action per hypothesis in parallel, and a merger reduces evidence and tracks executed/failed actions across iterations until enough signal is collected.

businessRules: The planner is bounded by a per-loop tool budget and must skip actions blocked by prior failures or exhaustion
Each planned action runs as its own LangGraph hypothesis branch via Send so they execute concurrently with bounded retries
Tool failures are classified as retryable vs non-retryable and exhausted action names are excluded from subsequent loops
The investigation loop terminates when the routing predicate observes enough evidence or the iteration cap is reached
crossDomainInteractions: Calls into Tool & Integration Surface to actually query Datadog/Grafana/EKS/etc.
Pulls credentials and instance metadata from Onboarding & Configuration via the resolved-integrations bag
Hands accumulated evidence to Root-Cause Diagnosis when planning concludes

nodes.name: Root-Cause Diagnosis & Findings Publishing
nodes.summary: Closes the investigation loop: an LLM diagnosis node turns gathered evidence into validated claims and a non-speculative root-cause sentence, then publish_findings formats a Slack Block Kit report, opens a GitLab MR comment, and dispatches notifications to Slack/Discord/Telegram and the Tracer ingest endpoint.

businessRules: Every claim emitted by the diagnosis LLM must be validated against concrete evidence; unsupported claims are bucketed separately and a 0-1 validity score is computed
When the evidence checker decides the subject is healthy, a healthy-verdict diagnosis short-circuits the report; insufficient evidence yields an inconclusive verdict listing missing vendor sources
The published report must include cited evidence with deep links to the original logs/metrics/traces and must respect masking before delivery
Slack delivery selects bot-token, hosted webapp proxy, or Incoming Webhook depending on which credentials are present
crossDomainInteractions: Consumes evidence and executed-hypothesis ledger from Incident Investigation
Sends tools-call summaries and claim provenance back to Tool & Integration Surface for citation IDs
Routes deliveries via Onboarding & Configuration's stored Slack/Discord/Telegram/Tracer credentials
Notifies Deployment & Runtime when self-investigation produced a result so it can write back to the originating thread

nodes.name: Tool & Integration Surface
nodes.summary: The auto-discovered registry of 100+ vendor tools (Datadog, Grafana, EKS, Kubernetes, GitHub, GitLab, Sentry, MongoDB, Vercel, Lambda, Honeycomb, etc.) plus their per-vendor service clients. Tools are exposed both to the investigation planner and to the conversational chat sub-graph.

businessRules: Tools auto-register on import — modules under app/tools/ are scanned, RegisteredTool instances collected, deduplicated, and cached as a registry snapshot
Every tool declares input_schema, surface (investigation/chat), required integrations, and outputs so the planner can reason about applicability
Tools must reuse shared service clients in app/services/ rather than inlining HTTP/SDK calls
Diagnostic-code execution is sandboxed via a bounded subprocess timeout
crossDomainInteractions: Invoked by Incident Investigation's executor for planned actions
Invoked by Alert Ingestion when Vercel/Slack pollers need to fetch deployment metadata
Reads credentials and instance metadata from Onboarding & Configuration's integration store
Audited by Guardrails & Safety when tool inputs/outputs traverse user-facing surfaces

nodes.name: Onboarding & Configuration
nodes.summary: The interactive opensre wizard that picks an LLM provider, validates credentials with a live completion, runs per-vendor _configure_<service> helpers, persists v2 multi-instance integration records into a local store, and re-verifies them with the central verify_integrations orchestrator.

businessRules: Provider credentials must validate with a live tiny-completion call before being persisted to the wizard store
Integrations are stored in a v2 multi-instance schema; legacy v1 records are migrated transparently on read
Every configured integration must have a verifier registered in app/integrations/verify.py — the verify-integrations CLI exits non-zero if any fail
Local Grafana onboarding spins up a docker-compose stack and seeds Loki with synthetic logs to give a working demo
crossDomainInteractions: Feeds resolved integration credentials into Incident Investigation via the resolve_integrations LangGraph node
Feeds vendor credentials into Tool & Integration Surface so registered tools can authenticate
Provides Slack/Discord/Telegram tokens to Root-Cause Diagnosis & Findings Publishing for delivery
Provides deploy targets to Deployment & Runtime

nodes.name: Deployment & Runtime
nodes.summary: The hosted FastAPI runtime (app/remote/server.py) that exposes /investigate, /investigate_stream, /health, and Discord-interactions endpoints; plus the deploy CLI that ships the agent to AWS EC2, Railway, Vercel, or LangSmith. Includes the Vercel deployment poller that auto-files investigations on failed deploys.

businessRules: Every deploy validates provider credentials (AWS role_arn or static keys, Railway api_token, Vercel token) before issuing the deploy
Deployed runtimes must pass /health then /ok polling within a bounded retry budget before they are considered ready
The Vercel poller dedupes processed deployment signatures across restarts so the same failure does not re-trigger an investigation
FastAPI investigate endpoints are gated by an optional X-API-Key dependency when REMOTE_API_KEY is configured
crossDomainInteractions: Hosts the Incident Investigation pipeline behind HTTP and SSE streams
Receives Vercel-derived alerts and forwards them to Alert Ingestion
Reads deploy targets and provider credentials from Onboarding & Configuration
Self-investigates its own runtime by building a runtime alert payload via remote/runtime_alert.py

nodes.name: Guardrails, Masking & Safety
nodes.summary: The cross-cutting safety layer: a regex/keyword guardrail engine that REDACTs or BLOCKs sensitive text with append-only JSONL audit logging, a masking context that reversibly substitutes IPs/emails/AWS resource IDs, JWT-based auth for the LangGraph runtime, and a sandboxed Python runner for diagnostic-code tools.

businessRules: Chat messages must pass through guardrails before reaching the LLM; BLOCK actions raise GuardrailBlockedError carrying offending rule names
Sensitive identifiers are replaced with reversible placeholders before LLM calls and restored on output
Every authenticated LangGraph thread must carry a verified Bearer JWT (Clerk-issued); auth context is injected into state by inject_auth_node
Diagnostic Python code only executes inside subprocess.run with a bounded timeout
crossDomainInteractions: Wraps every Tool & Integration Surface invocation that touches user input
Masks evidence and the diagnosis payload prior to Findings Publishing delivery
Authenticates every Deployment & Runtime request that hits LangGraph
Runs as a dedicated CLI surface (opensre guardrails) under Onboarding & Configuration

nodes.name: CLI Investigate
nodes.summary: Run an investigation locally from the opensre investigate CLI: parse argv, load a payload (file/stdin/sample), build alert context, and invoke the LangGraph pipeline.

nodes.name: Parse CLI args
nodes.summary: Translate Click options into the legacy argv list consumed by run_investigation_cli for backward compatibility.

nodes.name: Load alert payload
nodes.summary: Load alert payloads from files (JSON/YAML/Markdown), stdin, or interactive entry, falling back to bundled sample templates.

nodes.name: Resolve investigation context
nodes.summary: Build the canonical alert-name/pipeline-name/severity tuple from the raw alert and any CLI overrides.

nodes.name: Dispatch LangGraph pipeline
nodes.summary: Call run_investigation with keyword arguments inferred from the payload and CLI options; choose blocking vs streaming based on TTY.

nodes.name: Render result
nodes.summary: Print the formatted result with rich, return an exit code, and optionally emit JSON for scripting.

nodes.name: Vercel Deployment Polling
nodes.summary: Background poller that watches Vercel projects for failed deployments, dedupes by signature, builds a canonical alert from runtime logs and events, and dispatches an investigation.

nodes.name: Iterate Vercel projects
nodes.summary: Loop over the configured Vercel project allowlist on the configured interval.

nodes.name: Fetch deployment bundle
nodes.summary: Concurrently fetch deployment metadata, events, and runtime logs for a Vercel deployment.

nodes.name: Select actionable failure
nodes.summary: Pick the most recent deployment that represents an actionable failure suitable for opening an investigation candidate.

nodes.name: Dedupe by signature
nodes.summary: Use the persistent VercelPollStateStore to skip deployments whose signatures have already been processed.

nodes.name: Dispatch investigation
nodes.summary: Build the canonical alert dict and forward it to the investigation pipeline.

nodes.name: Extract Alert (LangGraph node)
nodes.summary: First node in the LangGraph pipeline: detect payload shape, call the LLM extractor for AlertDetails, fall back to deterministic parsing on failure, enrich the raw_alert, and emit a problem_md summary.

nodes.name: Detect payload shape
nodes.summary: Heuristically decide whether the raw alert is rich enough to require full-JSON serialization for the LLM extractor.

nodes.name: LLM extract details
nodes.summary: Call the configured LLM with the alert payload to produce a structured AlertDetails object.

nodes.name: Deterministic fallback
nodes.summary: Build an AlertDetails object directly from raw fields when the LLM call fails or returns an unusable response.

nodes.name: Enrich raw alert
nodes.summary: Merge extracted fields (alert_name, namespace, severity, etc.) back into the raw alert dict for later nodes.

nodes.name: Emit problem markdown
nodes.summary: Build the human-readable problem markdown summary that downstream nodes use to drive prompts.

nodes.name: Discord Slash-Command Trigger
nodes.summary: Discord interaction webhook that verifies the signature, dispatches a background investigation task, runs the agent pipeline against the supplied alert, and posts the resulting RCA back to the Discord channel.

nodes.name: Verify Discord signature
nodes.summary: Validate the Ed25519 signature on the incoming Discord interaction using the configured public key.

nodes.name: Dispatch background task
nodes.summary: Hand the interaction off to a background task so the webhook can ack quickly.

nodes.name: Run investigation
nodes.summary: Run the agent pipeline against the supplied alert payload via _execute_investigation.

nodes.name: Post result back to Discord
nodes.summary: Post the resulting RCA summary back into the originating Discord channel.

nodes.name: Plan Investigation Actions
nodes.summary: Planner orchestrator: detects sources from the alert, extracts keywords, seeds default actions per source, builds the planner LLM prompt with executed-history and tool budget, and selects the next action set.

nodes.name: Detect sources
nodes.summary: Inspect the raw alert, alert context, and resolved integrations to decide which observability sources are relevant.

nodes.name: Extract keywords
nodes.summary: Pull candidate identifiers (services, namespaces, error tokens) from problem markdown to seed downstream queries.

nodes.name: Seed default actions
nodes.summary: Build the seed action list from available action names plus source-specific seeds, ensuring a non-empty plan baseline.

nodes.name: Build planner prompt
nodes.summary: Compose the planner LLM prompt from problem markdown, executed-hypothesis history, available actions, and tool budget.

nodes.name: Plan with LLM
nodes.summary: Call the planner LLM with the constructed prompt and structured plan_model schema, returning the raw plan.

nodes.name: Apply tool budget
nodes.summary: Truncate the planner-proposed action list to fit within the configured tool budget for the current loop.

nodes.name: Detect reroute trigger
nodes.summary: Decide whether the investigation should be rerouted to a different source set based on prior evidence.

nodes.name: Investigate Hypothesis (Parallel Fan-Out)
nodes.summary: LangGraph fans out one hypothesis branch per planned action via Send. Each branch executes its tool with bounded retries and classifies failures so subsequent loops can avoid exhausted actions.

nodes.name: Send per-action branch
nodes.summary: LangGraph fans out one branch per planned action via Send so they execute concurrently.

nodes.name: Execute with retry
nodes.summary: Run a single tool action with bounded retries and exponential backoff for transient errors.

nodes.name: Classify failure
nodes.summary: Mark each failure as retryable or non-retryable so the planner can avoid exhausted actions next loop.

nodes.name: Track hypothesis
nodes.summary: Build an ExecutedHypothesis record and merge it into the running ledger of executed actions.

nodes.name: Merge Hypothesis Results
nodes.summary: Reducer node that merges per-hypothesis evidence back into shared state, post-processes vendor-specific tool outputs into a unified evidence schema, and routes the loop or transitions to diagnosis.

nodes.name: Gather branch results
nodes.summary: Collect the per-hypothesis outputs returned by the parallel investigate branches.

nodes.name: Post-process tool output
nodes.summary: Normalize vendor-specific tool results (Datadog, Honeycomb, Coralogix, EKS, Vercel, Lambda, etc.) into the unified evidence schema.

nodes.name: Dedupe and merge evidence
nodes.summary: Merge new evidence into the existing store, deduplicating by stable identity.

nodes.name: Build evidence summary
nodes.summary: Compile a compact LLM-friendly summary of accumulated evidence for the next planner loop.

nodes.name: Route loop vs diagnose
nodes.summary: Routing predicate decides whether to continue the planner loop or transition to diagnosis.

nodes.name: Resolve Integrations
nodes.summary: Pre-planner node that decodes auth, queries the remote tracer client for org integrations when available, falls back to the local store, and exposes the configured integrations bag to downstream nodes.

nodes.name: Decode auth context
nodes.summary: Extract the JWT auth claims from RunnableConfig as injected by inject_auth_node.

nodes.name: Try remote tracer
nodes.summary: Query the remote tracer client for org-level integrations when an authenticated session is available.

nodes.name: Fallback to local store
nodes.summary: Load integration configs from the local catalog/store and record provenance.

nodes.name: Expose integrations bag
nodes.summary: Place the resolved integrations dict into agent state for the planner and tool layer.

nodes.name: Diagnose Root Cause
nodes.summary: Diagnosis node: checks evidence sufficiency, short-circuits to healthy/inconclusive verdicts when applicable, otherwise builds a diagnosis prompt, invokes the LLM, validates each claim against evidence, and emits a 0-1 validity score.

nodes.name: Check evidence sufficiency
nodes.summary: Inspect the evidence dictionary to determine whether enough signal exists across logs/metrics/traces.

nodes.name: Short-circuit healthy/inconclusive
nodes.summary: Return a healthy or inconclusive verdict directly when evidence is missing or the subject is clearly healthy.

nodes.name: Build diagnosis prompt
nodes.summary: Compose a long-form diagnosis prompt from alert metadata, evidence, memory, and failure context.

nodes.name: Invoke diagnosis LLM
nodes.summary: Call the diagnosis LLM and parse the structured claim list it produces.

nodes.name: Validate claims against evidence
nodes.summary: Bulk-validate claims, splitting them into validated and non-validated buckets and attaching evidence references.

nodes.name: Score validity
nodes.summary: Compute a numeric 0-1 validity score from the ratio and weights of validated vs non-validated claims.

nodes.name: Return diagnosis state
nodes.summary: Emit the diagnosis state delta consumed by publish_findings.

nodes.name: Publish Findings
nodes.summary: Terminal node that builds the Slack mrkdwn / Block Kit report from the normalized ReportContext, applies masking, renders the report to terminal, and dispatches deliveries to Slack/Discord/Telegram plus the Tracer ingest endpoint.

nodes.name: Build report context
nodes.summary: Convert investigation state into a fully-populated ReportContext including evidence catalog and provenance.

nodes.name: Format Slack message
nodes.summary: Assemble the canonical Slack mrkdwn message body composing root cause, claims, evidence, and infra correlation.

nodes.name: Build Block Kit payload
nodes.summary: Construct the Slack Block Kit JSON with header, sections, and context blocks for richer rendering.

nodes.name: Apply masking
nodes.summary: Run masking over the report so sensitive identifiers do not leak in delivered content.

nodes.name: Render terminal report
nodes.summary: Pretty-print the report via rich (or plain text) for the operator running the investigation locally.

nodes.name: Dispatch deliveries
nodes.summary: Send the formatted report to Slack, Discord, Telegram, and any other configured channel.

nodes.name: Send ingest payload
nodes.summary: Forward the normalized investigation result to the Tracer ingest endpoint via the shared delivery transport.

nodes.name: GitLab MR Write-Back
nodes.summary: When the originating alert references a GitLab MR, post the RCA summary as a comment on that MR using the configured GitLab token.

nodes.name: Detect MR metadata
nodes.summary: Inspect the originating alert for GitLab MR identifiers (project, MR IID).

nodes.name: Build MR comment
nodes.summary: Render the RCA summary as a markdown comment suitable for posting to GitLab.

nodes.name: Post comment
nodes.summary: POST the comment to the GitLab MR via the configured GitLab token.

nodes.name: Slack Report Delivery
nodes.summary: Top-level Slack dispatcher that picks among bot-token, hosted webapp proxy, and Incoming Webhook based on configured credentials, then posts the formatted message and replies in-thread.

nodes.name: Select transport
nodes.summary: Choose among bot-token (chat.postMessage), hosted webapp proxy, or Incoming Webhook based on configured credentials.

nodes.name: Build action blocks
nodes.summary: Construct Slack Block Kit actions blocks (e.g. View Investigation buttons) given the investigation URL/ID.

nodes.name: Post message
nodes.summary: Issue the actual Slack POST through the selected transport.

nodes.name: Handle thread reply
nodes.summary: Honor thread_ts when set so RCA replies attach to the originating Slack thread.

nodes.name: Tool Registry Discovery
nodes.summary: On first call, scans every module under app/tools/, collects RegisteredTool instances (BaseTool subclasses or @tool-decorated functions), deduplicates them, caches the snapshot, and filters by surface for the caller.

nodes.name: Scan tool modules
nodes.summary: Import every module under app/tools/ so RegisteredTool instances become collectible.

nodes.name: Collect RegisteredTool instances
nodes.summary: Walk each module's public members for BaseTool subclasses and @tool-decorated functions.

nodes.name: Dedupe and cache
nodes.summary: Deduplicate by tool name and cache the registry snapshot via lru_cache.

nodes.name: Filter by surface
nodes.summary: Return tools filtered for either the investigation surface or the chat surface.

nodes.name: Execute Tool Action
nodes.summary: Resolves the tool referenced by a planned action, authenticates via the underlying service client, invokes the vendor API, normalizes the output via vendor-specific mappers, and returns an ActionExecutionResult.

nodes.name: Resolve tool
nodes.summary: Look up the RegisteredTool referenced by the planned action name.

nodes.name: Authenticate via service client
nodes.summary: Use the per-vendor service client to obtain credentials and a configured HTTP/SDK client.

nodes.name: Invoke vendor API
nodes.summary: Issue the actual API call (Datadog, Grafana, EKS, GitHub, etc.) through the service client.

nodes.name: Normalize output
nodes.summary: Apply vendor-specific mapping (e.g. _map_datadog_investigate, _map_eks_deployment_status) to convert raw output into the unified evidence schema.

nodes.name: Return ActionExecutionResult
nodes.summary: Wrap the success flag, returned data, and any error string in an ActionExecutionResult dataclass.

nodes.name: Conversational Tool Loop
nodes.summary: The chat sub-graph: applies guardrails to messages, routes between general chat and the tool-using agent, invokes the tool-bound LLM, runs queued tool calls, and returns ToolMessage results.

nodes.name: Apply guardrails
nodes.summary: Run the GuardrailEngine across user-authored chat messages and rewrite or block them before they reach the LLM.

nodes.name: Route general vs tool
nodes.summary: Router node decides between general chat and the tool-using chat agent based on intent.

nodes.name: Invoke tool-bound LLM
nodes.summary: Build and tool-bind the chat LLM (OpenAI/Anthropic only) and invoke it.

nodes.name: Run tool executor
nodes.summary: Execute queued tool calls produced by the chat agent and return ToolMessage results.

nodes.name: Return tool message
nodes.summary: Surface the ToolMessage results back to the chat sub-graph for the next agent step.

nodes.name: Onboarding Wizard
nodes.summary: End-to-end interactive onboarding: probe targets, prompt provider/model, validate credentials with a live completion, pick integrations, run per-vendor _configure_* helpers, persist the wizard store and .env, and render a summary panel.

nodes.name: Probe local targets
nodes.summary: Reachability probes that check the local store and any pre-installed integrations.

nodes.name: Select provider and model
nodes.summary: Drive provider/model selection from the curated ProviderOption catalog.

nodes.name: Validate provider credentials
nodes.summary: Issue a tiny live completion to validate the provider's API key before persisting.

nodes.name: Select integrations
nodes.summary: Let the user pick which observability/cloud integrations to configure now.

nodes.name: Configure each integration
nodes.summary: Iterate the selected integrations and dispatch to per-service _configure_* helpers, accumulating outcomes.

nodes.name: Persist wizard store and env
nodes.summary: Write the wizard store JSON and synchronize the project .env with provider-specific values.

nodes.name: Render saved summary
nodes.summary: Render the post-wizard summary panel showing chosen provider/model, store path, and successfully configured integrations.

nodes.name: Configure Vendor Integration
nodes.summary: Per-vendor configuration helper: prompt for credentials, normalize them into a Pydantic config model, run a live validate call, upsert the v2 instance into the integration store, and emit analytics.

nodes.name: Prompt for credentials
nodes.summary: Use questionary prompts to gather vendor-specific fields (host, token, region, etc.).

nodes.name: Normalize config
nodes.summary: Build the normalized Pydantic config model for the vendor.

nodes.name: Validate live
nodes.summary: Hit the vendor's auth/identity endpoint to confirm the credentials work.

nodes.name: Upsert v2 instance
nodes.summary: Persist the normalized config as a named v2 instance in the integration store.

nodes.name: Emit analytics
nodes.summary: Capture an integration-configured PostHog event for the analytics provider.

nodes.name: Verify Integrations
nodes.summary: Central verification orchestrator: list stored integrations, dispatch per-vendor verifiers, format results for CLI output, and compute a non-zero process exit code when anything failed.

nodes.name: List stored integrations
nodes.summary: Read the integration store and enumerate configured services and instances.

nodes.name: Dispatch per-vendor verifier
nodes.summary: Walk all configured integrations and dispatch to the matching _verify_<vendor> helper.

nodes.name: Format results table
nodes.summary: Format the aggregated results into a CLI-friendly table consumed by the wizard and the make target.

nodes.name: Compute exit code
nodes.summary: Compute a non-zero process exit code so CI pipelines fail when any integration is broken.

nodes.name: opensre doctor
nodes.summary: Diagnostic command that checks LLM provider configuration, lists configured integrations, compares the installed version with the latest release, and exits non-zero if any check fails.

nodes.name: Check LLM provider
nodes.summary: Verify that at least one LLM provider key is configured and reachable.

nodes.name: Check integrations
nodes.summary: Report which observability integrations have stored credentials.

nodes.name: Check version freshness
nodes.summary: Compare the installed opensre version with the latest published GitHub Release.

nodes.name: Render and exit
nodes.summary: Aggregate check results, render a Rich summary, and exit non-zero on any failure.

nodes.name: Remote /investigate HTTP
nodes.summary: FastAPI investigate endpoint: authenticate the request, normalize the InvestigateRequest payload, run the LangGraph pipeline (blocking or SSE-stream), persist the saved investigation, and respond.

nodes.name: Authenticate request
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency validates the X-API-Key header against REMOTE_API_KEY when configured.

nodes.name: Normalize InvestigateRequest
nodes.summary: Validate the inbound request via the InvestigateRequest Pydantic model.

nodes.name: Run pipeline
nodes.summary: Invoke run_investigation (blocking) or astream_investigation (SSE) on the precompiled LangGraph singleton.

nodes.name: Persist investigation
nodes.summary: Save the investigation result as JSON under the configured investigations directory.

nodes.name: Respond or stream
nodes.summary: Return the InvestigateResponse JSON or stream node/tool events as SSE depending on the endpoint.

nodes.name: opensre deploy
nodes.summary: Interactive deploy wizard that picks a backend (EC2/Railway/LangSmith), validates provider config (AWS role/keys, Railway api_token, etc.), runs the backend deploy, polls /health then /ok with bounded retries, and persists the remote URL.

nodes.name: Select deploy backend
nodes.summary: Interactive wizard picks among EC2, Railway, and LangSmith deploys.

nodes.name: Validate provider config
nodes.summary: Run provider-specific validators (AWS role/keys, Railway api_token, Vercel token) before issuing the deploy.

nodes.name: Run backend deploy
nodes.summary: Shell out to the provider CLI (railway up, langgraph build, EC2 provisioning) and capture results.

nodes.name: Poll health endpoint
nodes.summary: Poll /health then /ok on the deployed runtime with bounded retries until ready or timeout.

nodes.name: Persist remote URL
nodes.summary: Write the discovered remote URL into the wizard store so subsequent CLI invocations can reach it.

nodes.name: Deep Health Check
nodes.summary: Runtime self-monitoring endpoint that aggregates LLM connectivity, disk free, memory pressure, and instance metadata into a structured response.

nodes.name: Run LLM probe
nodes.summary: Issue a tiny LLM connectivity probe and record latency/status.

nodes.name: Check disk free
nodes.summary: Inspect disk free-space and report a DeepHealthCheck record.

nodes.name: Check memory pressure
nodes.summary: Cross-platform memory pressure probe (reads /proc/meminfo on Linux or vm_stat on macOS).

nodes.name: Aggregate response
nodes.summary: Collect every DeepHealthCheck record into the deep health endpoint response.

nodes.name: Runtime Self-Investigation
nodes.summary: When the deployed runtime senses something wrong, it probes local deployment health and remote ops status, builds a runtime alert payload, and dispatches an investigation against itself.

nodes.name: Probe local deployment health
nodes.summary: Probe the local deployment health endpoints to detect issues with the running runtime.

nodes.name: Probe remote ops status
nodes.summary: Use the remote-ops provider abstraction to ask Railway/EC2 about service status and recent logs.

nodes.name: Build runtime alert payload
nodes.summary: Combine local health, remote ops status, and Slack thread context into a normalized alert.

nodes.name: Dispatch self-investigation
nodes.summary: Send the runtime alert through the same investigation pipeline used for external alerts.

nodes.name: Guardrail Scan & Audit
nodes.summary: Loads YAML rules into compiled regex/keyword sets, scans inbound text, REDACTs matches in place or BLOCKs the call (raising GuardrailBlockedError), and writes an append-only JSONL audit entry.

nodes.name: Load rules
nodes.summary: Load YAML guardrail rules from the configured path and compile their regex patterns.

nodes.name: Apply patterns
nodes.summary: Scan inbound text against compiled regex/keyword patterns and collect ScanMatch records.

nodes.name: Redact or block
nodes.summary: Apply rule actions: REDACT replaces matches in place; BLOCK raises GuardrailBlockedError carrying offending rule names.

nodes.name: Audit JSONL
nodes.summary: Write an append-only JSONL audit entry for every evaluation via AuditLogger.

nodes.name: Return ScanResult
nodes.summary: Return the aggregated ScanResult to the caller so it can decide how to proceed.

nodes.name: Masking Roundtrip
nodes.summary: Detects sensitive identifiers (IPs, emails, AWS resource IDs), substitutes reversible placeholders before downstream LLM calls, then reverses placeholders on output to restore original values.

nodes.name: Detect identifiers
nodes.summary: Run built-in regex detectors for IPs, emails, AWS resource IDs, and other sensitive identifiers.

nodes.name: Substitute placeholders
nodes.summary: Substitute reversible placeholder tokens for each detected identifier.

nodes.name: Invoke downstream LLM
nodes.summary: Pass the masked text to the downstream consumer (LLM call, delivery, etc.).

nodes.name: Reverse placeholders on output
nodes.summary: Restore original identifiers in the response so consumers see the real values.

nodes.name: JWT Auth Injection
nodes.summary: LangGraph Auth integration: verify a Clerk-issued Bearer JWT, extract claims, and inject the resulting auth context into agent state for downstream nodes (inject_auth_node).

nodes.name: Verify Bearer token
nodes.summary: Verify the inbound Clerk-issued Bearer JWT using the configured JWKS.

nodes.name: Extract claims
nodes.summary: Decode the verified token into typed JWTClaims (subject, org, scopes, etc.).

nodes.name: Inject into state
nodes.summary: LangGraph node returns a state delta containing the auth context for downstream nodes.

edges.description: Hands the normalized AlertDetails and enriched raw_alert to the planner so investigation can begin.
The investigator executor invokes registered tools to query observability/cloud vendors for evidence.
Persisted credentials and instance metadata are read by tools and service clients at runtime.
resolve_integrations exposes the configured integrations bag to the planner so it knows which tools are runnable.
When evidence is sufficient, accumulated evidence and the executed-hypothesis ledger flow into the diagnosis node.
Slack/Discord/Telegram/Tracer delivery uses credentials persisted by the wizard.
The FastAPI runtime, Vercel poller, and Discord webhook hand inbound alerts to the ingestion pipeline.
The remote /investigate endpoints invoke the LangGraph pipeline directly.
Chat agent and planner inputs are scanned/masked by guardrails before LLM calls; auth is injected into state by inject_auth_node.
Reports are masked and audited prior to dispatch to Slack/Discord/Telegram.
Bearer JWTs are verified for every authenticated LangGraph thread served by the FastAPI runtime.
Diagnostic-code execution is sandboxed by app/sandbox/runner.py with a bounded subprocess timeout.
